features.details: Review existing sidebar component structure, CSS classes, and layout to understand the current implementation before making changes.
Create a hamburger menu button component that will be visible only on mobile devices. Position it appropriately in the header/navbar area.
Add CSS media queries to handle sidebar visibility and positioning on mobile devices. Include styles for collapsed state (hidden) and expanded state (visible overlay).
Add JavaScript/Vue logic to handle sidebar expand/collapse state. Include click outside to close, escape key to close, and state persistence if needed.
Implement CSS transitions for smooth sidebar open/close animations. Ensure animations work well on mobile devices without performance issues.

features.description: Add overlay backdrop for mobile sidebar
features.details: Create a semi-transparent backdrop overlay that appears when sidebar is expanded on mobile, allowing users to click outside to close the sidebar.
Test the sidebar behavior at various screen widths (320px, 375px, 414px, 768px, 1024px) to ensure proper responsive behavior.

features.description: Add unit tests for sidebar toggle functionality
features.details: Write unit tests to verify sidebar toggle functionality, including initial state, toggle action, and responsive behavior.

overall_approach: Add a hamburger menu toggle button for mobile devices, implement CSS media queries for responsive layout, and create JavaScript functionality to handle sidebar expand/collapse state with smooth animations.
potential_risks: Existing desktop layout may be affected by CSS changes
State management for sidebar visibility across page navigation
Animation performance on lower-end mobile devices
Z-index conflicts with other UI elements when sidebar is expanded
